reviews.review_msg: One of the best phil profs at SFU. Her lectures are so well-organized that you could get away with not doing the readings and still understand the material perfectly fine. Also a very reasonable marker who gives great feedback. The world would be a better place if more profs like Chelsea existed.

Good teacher, very clear with instructions. If you need an upper level elective gpa booster, phil 329 with Chelsea is the class. No need to attend lecture or read any of the readings. Just do a short writing assignment once a month, and two fairly short papers that are easy to write about and thats it. Marks easy too.
Took his class in fall 2025. He is an easy going professor, helps you alot with his feedback plus freebies to improve your grade. Really cares about what his students understand

Professor Tiffany is a great professor. Took this course as an upper division elective, and he makes philosophy easy to understand. This course requires lots of reading if you want to do well, and lectures are a MUST. Lots of small papers to write, and he drops 2 of the lowest quizzes. Midterm/final were easy if u studied well for quizzes!

Evan is a fantastic professor. This is the second course I have taken with him and I will be taking another next semester. He makes the material very accessible, despite it being very dense legal philosophy. His courses seem to be quite writing heavy (one essay a week for the first five weeks plus a term paper), but he offers great feedback/support

Evan is a top-notch professor. Very knowledgeable, with clear and fair expectations. Although he grades on a very steep scale, high marks were achievable if you focused during class and did the readings. Using Perusall as a communal reading platform was helpful and an easy grade booster.

Would recommend!
fantastic prof. super funny and helpful, fair grading and expectations. awesome class
